summary: Clear matrix mix with crisp highs backs a show that shines on Shakedown, Bertha and Fire while wrestling with off‑key moments.
review: The recording’s matrix blend (soundboard + audience) is generally clear and well balanced, offering bright highs, solid bass and only a faint high‑end hiss. Musically the concert is praised for strong first‑set energy and a laid‑back second set highlighted by Shakedown, Bertha, Fire and an inspired Morning Dew, though a few vocal missteps (Bobby’s Lovelight falsetto and Bob Weir’s post‑Space tuning) temper the enthusiasm.

ai_rating.rationale: Most reviewers give 4–5 stars, noting excellent sound and strong performances; a few low‑star comments focus on vocal slips, yielding an overall solid but not flawless rating.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Crisp highs and improved bass, slight high‑end hissiness, Phil a bit low in the mix; overall balanced and one of the better ’88 mixes.

show_quality.setlist_flow: Strong, fluid first set with Shakedown and solid jams; laid‑back second set that builds nicely despite a quick transition into Fire, delivering an overall relaxed yet engaging concert.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Praised for soaring licks on Shakedown, great tone throughout, and standout work on Queen Jane.
band_member_comments.Phil: Noted as slightly low in the mix but delivers nice bass highlights in several jams.
band_member_comments.Bob: Criticized for off‑key falsetto on Lovelight; however his MIDI experiments on Dew receive positive remarks.
band_member_comments.Keys: Brent’s Hammond B3 intro to GSL and piano work are highlighted as stellar, with a soaring synth/bass swirl toward the end of the show.
band_member_comments.Drums: Generally solid and well‑balanced; no specific complaints.
